changes: OpenClaw shipped v2026.4.1 on 2026-04-01, with a same-day beta alongside it, maintaining fast cadence from the category anchor.
ZeroClaw released v0.6.8 on 2026-04-02 after v0.6.7 on 2026-03-29, reinforcing its rapid Rust-native shipping pattern.
Hermes Agent reached v2026.3.30 and now explicitly includes a 'hermes claw migrate' path in its README, making the migration story from OpenClaw more overt.
PicoClaw elevated Android support into its visible product/news surface, widening the low-cost lightweight lane beyond boards and desktops.

TinyAGI has fully leaned into the TinyClaw -> TinyAGI rename in repo and release surface area, while NanoClaw, IronClaw, AstrBot, HiClaw, and SwarmClaw continue moving without forcing a taxonomy reset.
The monitored set still did not need to change: Hermes remains the canonical tracked repo at https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent, and the current positioning map still holds.
whyItMatters: The ecosystem is continuing to differentiate by durable wedges rather than collapsing into one generic OpenClaw clone pattern.
OpenClaw still sets the center of gravity, but ZeroClaw and PicoClaw keep validating the lightweight / low-cost lane as a serious branch of the market.
Hermes strengthens the adjacent threat/opportunity from the learning-loop side by making migration from OpenClaw more explicit.
TinyAGI’s rename is a reminder that orchestration players may increasingly frame themselves around teams/companies rather than assistant-clone branding.
This is meaningful enough for the daily record and outbound digest, but not enough to redraw the stable strategic read.
